summary: A compact two-story rectangular building with a flat roof, elevated on a concrete foundation. The structure features brick walls with horizontal stone/wood trim bands separating floors, multiple small windows with glass panes, and a dark roof cap. The building sits on a raised concrete platform with dirt surroundings.

shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along length axis; window placement suggests front-facing orientation

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: accent bands and trim between floors

elements: windows (approximately 4-5 per floor, small square panes)
rebuild_hints: Build 12×10 rectangular footprint with 2 floors, each approximately 3-4 blocks tall
Use brick as primary wall material for both floors
Add horizontal trim bands of stonebrick or wood planks at mid-height and between floors
Place small glass windows in regular pattern: approximately 4-5 windows per floor, evenly spaced
Create concrete foundation platform extending 1-2 blocks beyond building perimeter
Top with flat roof using dark wood or dark blocks
Ensure symmetry along the length axis for balanced appearance
Windows appear to be 1×1 or 2×2 blocks with glass panes and light-colored frames
uncertainties: Exact window dimensions and spacing pattern unclear from angles provided
Precise height of each floor level uncertain; estimate based on visual proportions
Specific trim material (stone vs. wood) difficult to distinguish; likely mixed
Interior layout and room configuration not visible
Exact depth dimension estimated from perspective; may vary by 1-2 blocks
Roof interior structure (solid vs. hollow) not determinable from exterior views
